title: Scope D T-D1: loadActiveMeta helper (strict + graceful variants) + knowledge_meta_auto_healed event
description: New module exposing two entry points: `loadActiveMeta(projectRoot)` (STRICT — throws on rebuild failure) and `loadActiveMetaOrStale(projectRoot)` (GRACEFUL — falls back to on-disk stale meta with degraded:true flag on rebuild failure). Internally: read on-disk meta → call buildKnowledgeMeta(projectRoot) → compare revision hashes → if mismatch, writeKnowledgeMeta to persist and return with auto_healed:true; if match, return on-disk with auto_healed:false. When auto-heal triggers, append knowledge_meta_auto_healed event to events.jsonl with previous_revision_hash + revision_hash + trigger:'read'.

files.change: Add new event shape `knowledge_meta_auto_healed` with fields: `previous_revision_hash: string`, `revision_hash: string`, `trigger: 'read'` (literal — currently only one trigger), `caller: 'planContext' | 'getKnowledgeSections' | 'getKnowledge' | 'extractKnowledge'` (optional but recommended for telemetry). Add to discriminated union at line 432.
Add round-trip parse fixture for knowledge_meta_auto_healed.

Export type `LoadActiveMetaResult = { meta: AgentsMeta; auto_healed: boolean; previous_revision_hash: string; revision_hash: string }`. Export type `LoadActiveMetaOrStaleResult = LoadActiveMetaResult & { degraded: boolean; error?: string }`. Export `async function loadActiveMeta(projectRoot, opts?: { caller?: string }): Promise<LoadActiveMetaResult>` — reads on-disk meta via readAgentsMeta, calls buildKnowledgeMeta to get derived, compares revisions; on mismatch calls writeKnowledgeMeta and returns auto_healed:true; on rebuild THROWS. Export `async function loadActiveMetaOrStale(projectRoot, opts?)` — same flow but rebuild failure → return on-disk meta + degraded:true + error:string. Both emit knowledge_meta_auto_healed event when auto-heal triggers (best-effort try/catch around appendEventLedgerEvent so telemetry can't break read path).

Tests: (1) fresh meta (revision matches derived) → no auto-heal, auto_healed:false, no event emitted; (2) stale meta (revisions differ) → auto-heal, writeKnowledgeMeta called once, event emitted with previous + new hash; (3) build failure under strict mode → throws; (4) build failure under OrStale → returns on-disk meta with degraded:true + error string; (5) auto-heal emits event with correct caller field when opts.caller supplied; (6) idempotent: second call after first auto-heal → no second heal (meta is now fresh).

implementation: Create load-active-meta.ts with both functions sharing internal logic
Wire knowledge_meta_auto_healed event emission via appendEventLedgerEvent (best-effort)
Write 6 unit tests covering all enumerated cases (use vitest mocks for buildKnowledgeMeta failure path)
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/server test load-active-meta` to confirm green

reference.examples: writeKnowledgeMeta result shape + readAgentsMeta helper provide the building blocks; event_ledger_truncated event shape at line 107 is the precedent for adding new events

criteria: Two named exports: loadActiveMeta (strict) and loadActiveMetaOrStale (graceful)
Strict mode throws on rebuild failure; graceful mode returns degraded:true
Fresh meta (revision matches derived) → auto_healed:false, no event emitted, no write
Stale meta → writeKnowledgeMeta called exactly once + knowledge_meta_auto_healed event emitted
Event payload includes previous_revision_hash, revision_hash, trigger:'read', and caller when supplied
6 unit tests green; schema round-trip green

rationale.chosen_approach: Two entry points (strict + graceful) — let each caller pick the right failure semantics
alternatives_considered: Single graceful entry (sections/get/extract risk silent corruption)
Single strict entry (planContext hint path becomes brittle)
decision_factors: planContext is best-effort hint — degradation acceptable
Sections/get/extract are authoritative — must fail loud
Symmetry with existing read/write split in knowledge-meta-builder
rationale.tradeoffs: Two functions to maintain (small cost; tests fully cover both branches)

manual_checks: Manually corrupt on-disk meta revision (edit string), trigger any read-side caller, observe heal + event in events.jsonl
